vehicles.slot: Slot at base of gear selector housing or column selector — pry/slide cover to expose
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Slot at base of gear selector housing or column selector — pry/slide cover to expose.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is under the rear seat — but use the jump terminals UNDER THE HOOD to connect your jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.notes: 4MATIC AWD: flatbed. AMG DCT: power preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4MATIC AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.

battery.location: In trunk (most generations) or under rear seat
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt on strut tower (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals ONLY. Never jump directly at the trunk battery. AGM jump pack required.

vehicles.slot: Slot near console or column selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Slot near console or column selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is under the rear seat — but use the jump terminals UNDER THE HOOD to connect your jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4MATIC AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.

battery.location: In trunk (most) or under rear seat
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals only. AGM jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Column selector (W220) or console selector (W221+) — slot at selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Column selector (W220) or console selector (W221+) — slot at selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is under the rear seat — but use the jump terminals UNDER THE HOOD to connect your jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4MATIC AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.

knownIssues.title: Air Suspension Drops Without Power
knownIssues.detail: Airmatic air suspension lowers to the bump stops when the vehicle loses power. This makes flatbed loading very difficult. Restore 12V power first if at all possible. On W220/W221, the suspension can be manually raised using a dealer tool if power cannot be restored.

knownIssues.title: Battery Location Varies
knownIssues.detail: Battery is under the rear seat on some generations, in the trunk on others. Do not assume under-hood location.

battery.location: Under rear seat (W220/W221) or in trunk (W222/W223)
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Air suspension drops with power loss. Use hood terminals only. AGM jump pack.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot near console selector base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood or in cargo area — varies by model. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Slot near console selector base.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — 4MATIC AWD is full-time, cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.

knownIssues.title: Full-Time 4MATIC AWD — Flatbed Only
knownIssues.detail: 4MATIC AWD is full-time with no 2WD mode on most GLC/GLE/GLS trims. These vehicles CANNOT be wheel-lifted without dollies. FLATBED is the standard and correct method for all 4MATIC vehicles.

knownIssues.title: Air Suspension on E-Class and Above Trims
knownIssues.detail: Higher trim GLE and GLS models may have AIRMATIC air suspension that drops without power. Check and restore power if possible before loading.

battery.location: Under hood or in cargo area — varies by model
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: 4MATIC AWD — flatbed only. Use hood terminals. AGM jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn the Mercedes-Benz EQC / EQE / EQS / EQA / EQB (Electric) to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Varies by model — check owner's manual for 12V auxiliary location. 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
vehicles.notes: Do NOT touch the high-voltage system.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. Never tow with driven wheels on ground.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY — Electric Drivetrain
knownIssues.detail: All EQ-series Mercedes are electric. Towing with wheels on ground will damage motors. No exceptions.

knownIssues.title: 800V System on EQS/EQE
knownIssues.detail: EQS and EQE use an 800V architecture. Extreme hazard — never probe or touch any HV components. 12V terminals only.

battery.location: Varies by model — check owner's manual for 12V auxiliary location
battery.jumpTerminals: 12V auxiliary terminals only — location varies by model
battery.warnings: 800V HV on EQS/EQE. NEVER touch HV components. 12V auxiliary only. Flatbed only.

vehicles.slot: Slot near selector or column shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Slot near selector or column shifter base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, passenger side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.notes: Large commercial van. Gas and diesel: same.
vehicles.warn: Large van — heavy wheel blocks required.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
DIESEL: Same shift-lock and tow procedures as gasoline variant. Heavier curb weight — account for extra weight in equipment ratings.

battery.warnings: Large commercial van. Heavy wheel blocks required.
